channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

  4. ☄️ Apophis: the "God of Chaos" returns and will be visible to the naked eye in 3 years  Techno-Science.net
  5. Space rock alert: Massive asteroid dwarfs Tunguska in 2029 approach  Kursiv Media

item.title: Ancient remains from Israel’s North show Neanderthal children grew faster than modern humans - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Ancient remains from Israel’s North show Neanderthal children grew faster than modern humans  Yahoo
  2. Neanderthal Kids Grew Up So Fast—at Least Compared With Their Human Peers—Thanks to Genetic Adaptations to Their Environment  Smithsonian Magazine
  3. Neanderthal toddlers grew faster than modern humans, probably because of the harsh environment they evolved in  Live Science
  4. Neanderthal babies grew so fast that at 6 months they already looked like modern toddlers  Earth.com
  5. Neanderthal babies grew faster than modern humans, study finds  Caliber.Az

item.title: Scientists Scan Gigantic Structure Hiding Behind Our Galaxy - Futurism
item.description:
1. Scientists Scan Gigantic Structure Hiding Behind Our Galaxy  Futurism
  2. Astronomers just mapped one of the largest structures in the universe, long hidden behind the Milky Way's 'Zone of Avoidance'  Live Science
  3. Astronomers Just Found A Colossal Structure Behind The Milky Way  The Daily Galaxy
  4. Giant hidden structure mapped behind the milky way's "dead zone"  Latest news from Azerbaijan
  5. Astronomers discovered a colossal structure hidden behind the Milky Way, 300 million light-years across, with a mass of 30 quadrillion Suns and at least 20 galaxy clusters.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: Evolution Keeps Making Crabs – But a Key Feature Has Only Evolved Once - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Evolution Keeps Making Crabs – But a Key Feature Has Only Evolved Once  ScienceAlert
  2. Researchers: Crabs began walking sideways approx. 200 million years ago  nhk.or.jp
  3. Everything May Be Evolving Into Crabs, But Their Sideways Walking Only Emerged Once  IFLScience
  4. Tuerkayana hirtipes (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!
  5. The Ancient Roots of the Crab Walk  Nautilus | Science

item.title: 100,000-Year-Old Neanderthal Fossils in Poland Reveal Unexpected Genetic Connections - SciTechDaily
item.description:

  2. New genetic evidence from Stajnia Cave reveals the oldest Neanderthal group reconstructed in Central-Eastern Europe  Phys.org
  3. News - Mitochondrial DNA Links Neanderthals in Poland’s Stajnia Cave  Archaeology Magazine
  4. Scientists Reconstruct One of Oldest Known Neanderthal Communities  Sci.News
  5. DNA analysis of Neanderthal teeth reveals a much closer genetic connection than previously known  Earth.com
